reasoning: The index value for Nov 23 will likely match the Nov 21 close (3575.57) plus minimal accrued interest. Over the past month, the index has traded in a choppy horizontal range roughly between 3560 and 3576. Currently trading at 3575.57, the index is at the top of its recent range. This local maximum makes an immediate short-term increase by Nov 30 less likely, as price mean-reversion tends to dominate daily coupon accrual over a 1-week horizon (P ~ 0.35). As the forecast horizon extends to December and February, the steady positive drift of the total return index—driven by coupon clipping (approx. 0.4% per month)—naturally increases the likelihood of posting a gain relative to the Nov 23 starting value. Consequently, the probability of exceeding the threshold rises over time. Concurrently, strictly interpreting the systemic instruction that uncertainty must increase with horizon (i.e., probabilities must move closer to 0.5 for distant dates), the progressively longer forecasts are scaled to smoothly approach 50%. This yields a probability progression of 0.35 for the 1-week horizon, 0.42 for the 1-month horizon, and 0.48 for the 3-month horizon.
